metric_value: One of three global Lotus NSPAP with unique combination of specialization and customization; acknowledged as innovative leader in global NSPAP business by competitors and customers
notes: Aberdeen validation of Interliant's market leadership in the NSPAP niche

metric_value: Over 2000 public and private customer organizations; over 20000 Lotus Notes databases hosted
notes: Quantitative scale metrics establishing Interliant's operational footprint

metric_value: Low up-front cost of connection; willingness to meet customer-specific requirements; network independence via multiple public carriers; global reach
notes: Four differentiators vs competing NSPAPs

metric_value: Economies of scale providing more robust/timely/complete services than most companies can provide internally
notes: Core outsourcing rationale: scale economics

metric_value: Technology leadership — Service Provider maintains expertise in latest software/hardware versions eliminating enterprise technology obsolescence
notes: Outsourcing benefit: perpetual technology refresh

metric_value: Time-to-market advantage — outsourcing notes hosting enables months of increased productivity without network provisioning headaches
notes: Customer quotes support this value claim

metric_value: Focus on core competencies — frees CIOs and MIS directors from network build-out complexity to focus on productivity benefits of Notes
notes: Core competency rationale for outsourcing; central theme throughout document

metric_value: Domino only web server application allowing user to add/delete files from web server via browser; enables Notes functionality for users without proprietary client
notes: Aberdeen highlights Domino's unique write-access web capability as key differentiator

metric_value: Lotus Notes Release 5 expected end of 1997 to further blur distinction between Notes and Domino; anticipated as significant enhancement
notes: Forward-looking product roadmap reference

metric_value: Dedicated: X.25; TDM/T/E carrier; Frame Relay. Dial-up: PPP; X.PC. Mobile: RAM/ARDIS wireless messaging — multi-protocol network independence
notes: Full connectivity matrix documented

metric_value: Conducts business through Notes integrator reseller program; win-win: integrators get complete service offering plus recurring revenue; Interliant gets distribution
notes: Channel strategy: indirect resale through Notes SIs

metric_name: Interliant long-term viability as Notes hosting pioneer
metric_value: Aberdeen concludes Interliant best-positioned to manage and develop virtual infrastructure integral to hosted application; recommends enterprise CIOs evaluate Interliant before building network empires
notes: Aberdeen's strongest positive recommendation for Interliant's future

metric_value: Interliant filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy August 5 2002; CEO/COO resigned; total debts $109.9M vs $5.77M cash; revenue fell 45% to $19.2M in final reported quarter
notes: Interliant failed despite Aberdeen's strong 1997 endorsement; dotcom boom/bust cycle ended its run

metric_value: NaviSite acquired Interliant assets in May 2003 for approximately $7M cash/credits; included managed messaging/collaboration/hosting/security businesses and UK operations
notes: Fire-sale price vs Interliant's former value; predecessor to modern MSP model

metric_value: IBM sold Notes/Domino to HCL Technologies in 2019; HCL Notes 14 released 2024; platform survives in niche legacy installed base but lost mainstream enterprise relevance
notes: Notes/Domino succeeded as platform longer than Interliant but ultimately marginalized by email/cloud collaboration tools

metric_name: Hosted application outsourcing as standard enterprise practice
metric_value: Aberdeen Group believes all organizations should consider outsourced solutions for some component of their IT and network activities
notes: Broad prediction about outsourcing becoming standard; stated as Aberdeen's position

metric_value: Cloud computing (AWS launched 2006; Azure 2010; GCP 2011) fulfilled and vastly exceeded Aberdeen's 1997 prediction that all organizations should consider IT outsourcing
notes: The category Aberdeen predicted did become universal; Interliant was merely early and under-resourced

metric_value: 24x7 customer support for network connectivity; Notes administration; security; maintenance; server maintenance; ISP-level infrastructure
notes: Service catalog documented — essentially what modern SaaS companies provide

metric_value: Interliant offers better value than other NSPAPs; has application management capabilities that non-specialized ISPs cannot match; succeeds where larger telecoms have failed
notes: Aberdeen endorsement of Interliant vs generic ISPs and telecoms

metric_value: Decision models often fail to factor in intangible services cost and technology investment required to remain technology leader; Interliant customers avoid technology obsolescence costs
notes: TCO argument for outsourcing: hidden costs of internal IT management
